introSection: Twisted Cyclone is a steel hybrid roller coaster located at Six Flags Over Georgia in Austell, Georgia, USA. It opened on May 25, 2018 and is a Rocky Mountain Construction I-Box Track conversion of Georgia Cyclone, which closed on July 30, 2017.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Georgia Cyclone closed on July 30, 2017. Its replacement, Twisted Cyclone, was announced on August 31, 2017.

The lead car for Twisted Cyclone was revealed at the 2017 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA.

The height was raised to 100 feet (30 m), the angle was steepened to 75 degrees, and a total of ten airtime hills and three inversions were added along the course of the ride. The trains had their theme modified to represent a 1960s-era sports convertible, and the maximum speed remained unchanged at 50 mph. The track was shortened to approximately 2,400 feet (730 m).

Construction of Twisted Cyclone officially began in the fall of 2017.

In the spring of 2018, construction was complete with the final track piece being installed. Testing of the attraction began in May 2018.

Twisted Cyclone opened to Six Flags season pass holders and members on May 24, 2018 and to the general public the following day.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Twisted Cyclone features the world's first reverse cobra roll element. This element is basically two zero-g rolls with a banked turn in the middle.

fullText:
Twisted Cyclone is a steel hybrid roller coaster located at Six Flags Over Georgia in Austell, Georgia, USA. It opened on May 25, 2018 and is a Rocky Mountain Construction I-Box Track conversion of Georgia Cyclone, which closed on July 30, 2017.

Georgia Cyclone closed on July 30, 2017. Its replacement, Twisted Cyclone, was announced on August 31, 2017.

The lead car for Twisted Cyclone was revealed at the 2017 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA.

The height was raised to 100 feet (30 m), the angle was steepened to 75 degrees, and a total of ten airtime hills and three inversions were added along the course of the ride. The trains had their theme modified to represent a 1960s-era sports convertible, and the maximum speed remained unchanged at 50 mph. The track was shortened to approximately 2,400 feet (730 m).

Construction of Twisted Cyclone officially began in the fall of 2017.

In the spring of 2018, construction was complete with the final track piece being installed. Testing of the attraction began in May 2018.

Twisted Cyclone opened to Six Flags season pass holders and members on May 24, 2018 and to the general public the following day.

Twisted Cyclone features the world's first reverse cobra roll element. This element is basically two zero-g rolls with a banked turn in the middle.
